_meta.notes: Concise, table-ready Eberron faith entries formatted for Pathway's PF2e deity lookup. These are not Archives of Nethys entries.

deities.name: Arawai
deities.summary: Sovereign Host deity of fertility, agriculture, wilderness, weather, and the cycle of natural life.
deities.description: Arawai is honored by farmers, druids, healers, sailors, and any who depend on the generosity and danger of the natural world.
deities.edicts: nurture growth, protect harvests and healthy families, respect the living world, offer shelter and food when able
deities.anathemas: blight fertile land, waste food during hardship, exploit animals or workers cruelly, destroy natural abundance for vanity

deities.name: Aureon
deities.summary: Sovereign Host deity of knowledge, law, magic, scholarship, and civilization's written traditions.
deities.description: Aureon is revered by judges, mages, teachers, scribes, and rulers who believe order and learning are sacred duties.
deities.edicts: preserve knowledge, teach the willing, uphold just law, use magic responsibly
deities.anathemas: burn libraries, falsify law for personal gain, suppress learning, use magic to enslave minds

deities.name: Balinor
deities.summary: Sovereign Host deity of beasts, hunting, the wild, and the balance between hunter and prey.
deities.description: Balinor is honored by hunters, scouts, wardens, trappers, and travelers who survive by reading the wild.
deities.edicts: hunt with respect, protect wild places, take only what is needed, test yourself against nature
deities.anathemas: slaughter wastefully, torment animals, poison the land, break the laws of fair chase

deities.name: Boldrei
deities.summary: Sovereign Host deity of community, home, marriage, shelter, and civic peace.
deities.description: Boldrei is honored at hearths, town halls, weddings, and anywhere people choose mutual protection over isolation.
deities.edicts: build community, protect homes and families, mediate disputes, welcome guests in good faith
deities.anathemas: betray hospitality, destroy a home out of spite, abandon dependents, inflame needless feuds

deities.name: Dol Arrah
deities.summary: Sovereign Host deity of honor, sunlight, sacrifice, and righteous war.
deities.description: Dol Arrah is revered by paladins, commanders, diplomats, and soldiers who seek victory without abandoning honor.
deities.edicts: fight honorably, defend the innocent, seek truth in conflict, sacrifice for a just cause
deities.anathemas: win through treachery, strike down the helpless, hide corruption behind noble words, betray a sworn duty

deities.name: Dol Dorn
deities.summary: Sovereign Host deity of courage, contests, physical strength, and battle.
deities.description: Dol Dorn is honored by soldiers, athletes, rebels, gladiators, and anyone who believes courage is proven through action.
deities.edicts: show courage, test your strength, protect companions in battle, celebrate worthy competition
deities.anathemas: cowardly abandon allies, cheat in honorable contests, bully the weak, refuse a fair challenge from fear

deities.name: Kol Korran
deities.summary: Sovereign Host deity of commerce, trade, travel, negotiation, and fair profit.
deities.description: Kol Korran is honored by merchants, bankers, negotiators, caravan masters, and people who believe exchange binds civilization together.
deities.edicts: trade fairly, honor contracts, reward enterprise, travel and connect distant communities
deities.anathemas: cheat partners, debase honest coin, sabotage peaceful trade, hoard wealth during desperate need

deities.name: Olladra
deities.summary: Sovereign Host deity of luck, plenty, joy, celebration, and unexpected mercy.
deities.description: Olladra is invoked by gamblers, innkeepers, cooks, travelers, and anyone hoping fortune smiles at the right moment.
deities.edicts: share good fortune, celebrate life, feed the hungry, take hopeful chances
deities.anathemas: spoil joy out of spite, cheat guests, refuse aid when abundance is easy, curse another's honest luck

deities.name: Onatar
deities.summary: Sovereign Host deity of craft, fire, invention, industry, and artisanship.
deities.description: Onatar is revered by smiths, artificers, engineers, wrights, and anyone who makes useful wonders with patient hands.
deities.edicts: create lasting works, teach craft, use fire as a tool not a terror, improve the lives of others through invention
deities.anathemas: destroy masterwork craft from jealousy, sabotage honest labor, waste rare materials, use invention only for cruelty

deities.name: The Devourer (Eberron)
deities.epithet: Lord of the Deep and Storm
deities.summary: Dark Six power of destructive nature, storms, flood, hunger, and the cruel sea.
deities.description: The Devourer is feared by sailors and coastal folk who know that nature gives and takes without pity.
deities.edicts: respect the fury of nature, take what you need by strength, survive flood and storm, humble those who think civilization masters the world
deities.anathemas: show contempt for the sea, deny hunger's power, spare a sworn enemy out of softness, build without regard for nature's wrath

deities.name: The Fury
deities.summary: Dark Six power of passion, revenge, madness, pain, and unleashed emotion.
deities.description: The Fury answers those who turn grief, desire, and humiliation into a fire that consumes all restraint.
deities.edicts: embrace passion, avenge deep wrongs, break chains of shame, make enemies feel what they caused
deities.anathemas: forgive a betrayal cheaply, numb yourself to feeling, submit quietly to humiliation, deny desire out of fear

deities.name: The Keeper
deities.summary: Dark Six power of greed, death, hoarding, secrets, and souls withheld from rest.
deities.description: The Keeper is whispered of by grave robbers, miser-kings, and those who would bargain anything to avoid loss.
deities.edicts: claim what others cannot keep, guard valuable secrets, profit from death, preserve what you own at any cost
deities.anathemas: give away treasure without advantage, reveal a profitable secret freely, let a debtor escape, honor the dead above the living

deities.name: The Mockery
deities.summary: Dark Six power of betrayal, dishonorable war, terror, treachery, and victory at any price.
deities.description: The Mockery is invoked by assassins, torturers, oathbreakers, and soldiers who believe mercy is a weakness enemies exploit.
deities.edicts: win by any means, exploit fear, punish weakness, make betrayal useful
deities.anathemas: risk victory for honor, spare a dangerous enemy, keep an oath that costs you power, show mercy to prove virtue

deities.name: The Shadow
deities.summary: Dark Six power of ambition, corruption, forbidden magic, monsters, and hidden knowledge.
deities.description: The Shadow tempts mages, monsters, and seekers of power who believe every secret deserves a price.
deities.edicts: seek hidden power, master forbidden lore, embrace what others call monstrous, turn fear into authority
deities.anathemas: destroy rare magic out of fear, refuse power because it is taboo, expose secrets without profit, submit to those who fear your nature

deities.name: The Traveler
deities.epithet: The Sovereign of Chaos and Change
deities.summary: Dark Six power of change, deception, invention, transformation, and dangerous freedom.
deities.description: The Traveler is honored by changelings, wanderers, inventors, outcasts, and anyone who survives by becoming something new.
deities.edicts: embrace change, cross boundaries, protect useful masks, reveal new possibilities through disruption
deities.anathemas: enforce stagnation, punish harmless transformation, trap people in assigned roles, refuse a necessary journey

deities.name: The Silver Flame
deities.summary: Faith of protection, sacrifice, exorcism, and the binding of supernatural evil.
deities.description: The Silver Flame is not a conventional god, but a sacred force and church devoted to protecting innocents from fiends, undead, lycanthropes, and corruption.
deities.edicts: protect innocents from supernatural evil, show discipline and mercy, sacrifice for the greater good, expose corruption
deities.anathemas: traffic with fiends or undead, abuse religious authority, abandon innocents to monsters, let zeal become cruelty

deities.name: The Blood of Vol
deities.summary: Faith centered on mortal divinity, blood, undeath, self-mastery, and defiance of death.
deities.description: The Blood of Vol teaches that divinity lies within mortal blood and that death is an enemy to be understood, delayed, or defeated.
deities.edicts: seek the divinity within, honor blood and sacrifice, master death's lessons, protect your community of the faithful
deities.anathemas: submit to fate, waste mortal potential, destroy useful undead out of superstition, betray sworn kin of blood or faith

deities.name: The Undying Court
deities.summary: Aereni ancestral faith venerating deathless elf counselors sustained by devotion and positive energy.
deities.description: The Undying Court is a host of deathless ancestors who guide Aerenal through memory, duty, and reverence for perfected lives.
deities.edicts: honor worthy ancestors, preserve memory, perfect your craft and conduct, defend Aerenal and its traditions
deities.anathemas: defile honored dead, embrace corrupt undeath, abandon ancestral duties, erase a lineage's memory

deities.name: The Path of Light
deities.summary: Kalashtar and Adaran faith devoted to discipline, compassion, dreams, and the eventual triumph over il-Lashtavar.
deities.description: The Path of Light teaches that spiritual discipline and compassion can turn the age toward a brighter dream.
deities.edicts: practice compassion and discipline, resist nightmare and tyranny, cultivate inner light, aid others on the path
deities.anathemas: feed despair, empower nightmare spirits, exploit dreams, abandon self-mastery for cruelty

deities.name: The Lord of Blades
deities.summary: Warforged messianic warlord revered by followers who seek a destiny apart from flesh-born nations.
deities.description: The Lord of Blades is a living figure rather than a distant god, but some warforged treat his cause with religious devotion.
deities.edicts: free warforged from servitude, build strength in the Mournland, reject those who would own the forged, prepare for inevitable war
deities.anathemas: submit willingly to flesh-born masters, destroy warforged potential, betray the forged to old nations, treat the Last War's chains as sacred

deities.name: The Becoming God
deities.summary: Warforged faith devoted to building or awakening a divine being from collective devotion and crafted relics.
deities.description: Followers of the Becoming God believe the forged can create, reveal, or assemble a divinity of their own.
deities.edicts: create sacred works, seek purpose beyond your maker, honor the souls of the forged, gather signs of emerging divinity
deities.anathemas: deny warforged souls, destroy holy craft, abandon the search for purpose, treat creation as ownership

deities.name: Cults of the Dragon Below
deities.summary: A catch-all for subterranean cults devoted to aberrations, daelkyr, overlords, madness, and buried powers.
deities.description: No two cults of the Dragon Below are quite alike; most are defined by obsession, revelation, transformation, or the voice of something imprisoned beneath the world.
deities.edicts: heed the voice below, uncover buried truths, transform flesh and mind, prepare the world for the power you serve
deities.anathemas: silence a true revelation, reject glorious transformation, expose the cult before its hour, place surface law above the call below
